data.title: Iranian Delegation's Visit to Pakistan Remains Unconfirmed
data.description: According to Odaily, Iran's Tasnim News Agency reported today, April 19, that there are unconfirmed reports about an Iranian delegation planning to visit Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan, next Tuesday, April 21. Official confirmation from Iran is still pending.

data.title: Institutional Investors Show Increased Interest in Digital Assets Amid Regulatory Clarity
data.description:
Institutional investors are displaying a growing interest in digital assets, driven by enhanced regulatory clarity and the introduction of new products. According to CoinDesk, a recent survey conducted by Nomura and Laser Digital highlights this positive shift in sentiment among institutional players. The survey indicates that clearer regulations are playing a crucial role in fostering confidence and encouraging deeper engagement with digital assets.

The findings suggest that as regulatory frameworks become more defined, institutional investors are more willing to explore opportunities within the digital asset space. This increased interest is further supported by the development of innovative products tailored to meet the needs of institutional clients. The survey underscores the importance of regulatory clarity in paving the way for broader adoption and integration of digital assets into traditional financial systems.

As the digital asset market continues to evolve, the role of institutional investors is becoming increasingly significant. Their participation not only brings credibility to the market but also contributes to its stability and growth. The survey results reflect a growing recognition of the potential benefits and opportunities that digital assets offer, prompting institutions to consider them as viable components of their investment strategies. This trend is expected to continue as regulatory environments mature and more products are introduced to cater to institutional demands.

data.title: Survey Reveals Growing Optimism for Crypto Among Japanese Investors
data.description: A recent survey conducted by Nomura and Laser Digital among over 500 investment professionals in Japan indicates a growing optimism towards cryptocurrency. According to NS3.AI, the study reveals that 65% of respondents consider crypto as a valuable portfolio diversifier. Additionally, 31% of those surveyed now hold a positive outlook on the future of crypto over the next year, marking an increase from 25% in 2024.

data.title: China's Financial Law Draft Consultation Concludes with Limited Crypto Focus
data.description: China's public consultation on the draft Financial Law concluded on April 19. According to NS3.AI, the draft provides limited attention to digital currencies and the regulatory boundaries of crypto assets. It proposes to enhance regulatory powers, allowing authorities to access records and freeze or seize suspected illegal funds or securities during investigations. Additionally, the draft includes measures to prevent suspected violators from leaving the country while investigations are ongoing.

data.title: ABC News: US Vice President Vance will lead a US delegation to Pakistan for talks with Iran.
data.description: According to ABC News: The U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations stated that Vice President Vance will lead a U.S. delegation to Pakistan for talks with Iran. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iran-Pakistan Diplomatic Meeting Speculation Grows
data.description:
On April 19, reports emerged from Iran's Tasnim News Agency regarding an Iranian delegation's potential visit to Islamabad, Pakistan, next Tuesday, April 21. According to BlockBeats, this information has not yet been confirmed by Iranian officials.

Despite the lack of official confirmation, PolyBeats monitoring indicates that the probability of a U.S.-Iran diplomatic meeting occurring before various dates is increasing on the prediction market Polymarket. The likelihood of a meeting happening before April 20 (Monday) stands at 85%, before April 21 (Tuesday) at 91%, and before April 22 (Wednesday) at 94%.

data.title: Iran has not confirmed the news of the resumption of talks between Iran and the United States.
data.description: According to Iran's Tasnim news agency today (April 19), reports that an Iranian delegation will travel to Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan, next Tuesday (April 21) have not yet been officially confirmed by Iranian officials. (CCTV International News)

data.title: The public consultation period for the draft Financial Law has ended, with minimal discussion on the legal status of digital currencies and the regulatory boundaries for crypto assets.
data.description: According to Caixin, the month-long public consultation period for the draft "Financial Law of the People's Republic of China" ended today (April 19). This is China's, and the world's, first overarching law specifically addressing finance. The expansion of "quasi-judicial powers" granted to financial regulators in the draft is a topic of great market interest. According to Article 55 and related provisions, financial regulatory authorities have the right to access and copy the property rights information, communication records, and transaction records of relevant entities and individuals when investigating financial violations; they can directly freeze or seize funds and securities if there is evidence of suspected transfer or concealment of illegal funds; and they can even decide that suspected parties cannot leave the country during the investigation. Furthermore, Zeng Gang, chief expert and director of the Shanghai Financial and Development Laboratory, believes that the "Financial Law" should also strengthen its focus on and coverage of emerging financial business models. Issues such as AI-driven financial decision-making, the legal status of digital currencies, and the regulatory boundaries of crypto assets—issues that have already sparked widespread debate globally—are largely absent from the draft. How to maintain a dynamic balance between legal regulation and inclusive innovation remains an unresolved challenge for the legislation.

data.title: Yi Lihua: Preserving principal is crucial in a bear market; be wary of on-chain theft incidents.
data.description: Yi Lihua posted on the X platform, stating that preserving principal is crucial in a bear market. On-chain theft is frequent, and many investors lose everything in pursuit of a few percentage points of profit, just like many past investment mistakes. All investments carry risk, even those held in exchanges or through mining. Our top principle now is to always have profit-taking and stop-loss orders; you must always consider how to handle the worst-case scenario. Otherwise, the most regrettable thing is losing your principal even when a bull market arrives. Be optimistic in investing, but be rigorous in risk management.

data.title: Investment Strategies Emphasized Amid Market Challenges
data.description:
In a recent post on the X platform, Yi Lihua highlighted the importance of preserving capital during bear markets. According to Odaily, Yi noted the frequent occurrences of on-chain thefts and stressed that the pursuit of marginal gains should not come at the expense of losing principal investments. He compared this to common investment mistakes made in the past, emphasizing that all investments carry risks, whether funds are held on exchanges or in financial mining.

Yi underscored the necessity of having clear strategies for profit-taking and loss-cutting, advising investors to always prepare for worst-case scenarios. He warned that failing to do so could result in missing out on opportunities when the market turns bullish. Yi concluded by advocating for an optimistic investment approach paired with stringent risk management.

data.title: U.S. Envoy to Visit Pakistan for Iran Talks
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that his special envoy, Steve Witkoff, will travel to Pakistan for discussions with Iran on Tuesday. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the diplomatic efforts aimed at addressing regional tensions. The visit underscores the ongoing engagement between the U.S. and Iran amid complex geopolitical dynamics.

data.title: Brent Crude Prices Remain High Amid Strait of Hormuz Disruption
data.description: Rishi Sunak highlighted the economic impact of the Iran war, noting that trade constitutes approximately 25% of the U.S. GDP compared to 60–70% for the UK. According to NS3.AI, this disparity leaves the UK and Europe more vulnerable to the economic consequences of the conflict. Sunak pointed out that Brent crude prices surged above $119 per barrel following disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz and continue to trade above $90 despite a two-week ceasefire.

data.title: Bitcoin Whale Activity: $3 Million USDC Deposit Fuels 30x Short Position
data.description: A significant transaction has been observed in the cryptocurrency market as a whale deposited $3 million USDC into HyperLiquid, subsequently increasing a 30x short position on Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, this move has resulted in a total position of 700 BTC, valued at approximately $52.89 million. The activity highlights ongoing market dynamics and investor strategies in the volatile crypto landscape.

data.title: Michael Saylor has released more information about Bitcoin Tracker, and may disclose data on his increased holdings next week.
data.description: Strategy founder Michael Saylor posted another update related to Bitcoin Tracker, captioned "Think Even ₿igger." Based on past patterns, Strategy typically discloses its Bitcoin holdings the day after such an update.

data.title: Trump: US delegation will travel to Pakistan tomorrow night; if Iran does not accept the agreement, we will "not be polite."
data.description: Trump posted on social media that Iran decided to fire in the Strait of Hormuz yesterday, a complete violation of our ceasefire agreement! Multiple shots were fired at a French ship and a British cargo ship. That's not friendly, is it? My representatives will be traveling to Islamabad, Pakistan; they will arrive there tomorrow night for negotiations. Iran recently announced its intention to close the strait, which is strange, since our blockade has already closed it. They've unwittingly helped us, and closing the waterway will only hurt them—$500 million a day! $500 million a day! We are offering a very fair and reasonable agreement, and I hope they accept it, because if they don't, the United States will destroy every power plant and every bridge in Iran. The days of being easy to talk to are over! They will crumble quickly, easily. If they don't accept the agreement, I will have the honor of doing what must be done. (Jin Shi)

data.title: A trader missed out on $2.6 million in ASTEROID profits.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, 80 days ago, trader 0x5811 spent $542 to buy 7.43 billion ASTEROID tokens. The day before ASTEROID's price surged, this trader sold all 7.43 billion ASTEROID tokens at $405, incurring a loss of $137. Today, the 7.43 billion ASTEROID tokens are worth over $2.6 million.

data.title: Michael Saylor Shares Bitcoin Tracker Update
data.description: On April 19, Michael Saylor, founder of Strategy, released new information regarding the Bitcoin Tracker. According to BlockBeats, Saylor encouraged followers to 'Think Even ₿igger.' Historically, Strategy has disclosed additional Bitcoin holdings the day after such announcements. As of the last disclosure, Strategy holds a total of 780,897 BTC, with an average acquisition cost of approximately $75,577 per Bitcoin.

data.title: SEC Approves FINRA Rule Change, Easing Day Trading Requirements
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has approved a rule change proposed by the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA) that significantly alters the requirements for pattern day traders. According to NS3.AI, the new rule eliminates the $25,000 minimum equity requirement for pattern day traders, reverting to the standard $2,000 margin-account baseline.

Additionally, the rule change removes the pattern day trader designation and replaces trade-count limits with intraday margin calculations based on real-time position risk. This adjustment aims to provide more flexibility for traders by allowing margin calculations to reflect current market conditions.

The full implementation of these changes may take up to 18 months, as brokerage firms will need to upgrade their systems to accommodate the new regulations. While the rule change does not directly affect cryptocurrency trading regulations, it could indirectly impact Bitcoin and other digital assets if the eased equity speculation rules increase retail investors' risk appetite.

data.title: Trump says a deal between the US and Iran is still possible: it will happen.
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump told ABC News on the 19th that Iran had "seriously violated" the ceasefire, but he still believed a peace agreement could be reached with Iran. "It will happen. Either way. Either it's negotiable or it's tough. It will happen. You can quote me." (Xinhua)

data.title: A whale deposited $3 million USDC into HyperLiquid and increased its BTC short position by 30x.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited $3 million USDC into HyperLiquid and increased its short position in BTC (30x). It currently holds 700 BTC, worth $52.89 million, with an entry price of $75,119 and a liquidation price of $80,839.93.
